title: Smoky Canadian Gratin Casserole
description: A hearty, smoky casserole made with roasted Canadian bacon, creamy cheese, and a crispy gratin topping.
instructions: In a large skillet, cook Canadian bacon over medium-high heat until browned, about 5 minutes. Set aside.
In the same skillet, add onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
In a separate saucepan, combine shredded cheese and 1/2 cup milk. Heat over low heat, stirring until cheese is melted and smooth.

In a greased 9x13-inch baking dish, arrange a layer of potatoes. Top with a layer of cooked bacon, followed by a layer of onion mixture, and finally a layer of cheese sauce. Repeat this process until all ingredients are used, finishing with a layer of cheese sauce on top.
Dust the top of the casserole with flour and sprinkle with gratin topping.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the casserole is hot and bubbly.
Let the casserole rest for 10 minutes before serving.
